title: Evangelion Opening Credits taken from another show
display_title: Evangelion Opening Credits taken from another show
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Opening Credits taken from another show?             Quote Post by The Goose » Fri Jan 04, 2008 6:38 pm On the opening credits for Eva are they, in anyway, inspired by the opening credits of Gerry Anderson's sci-fi tv show, UFO? They're both quick paced that show images of the characters, the date the show takes place, and machines. Here are the two openings. Look at them back to back to see if I'm right or just nuts. Eva: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EgATlhZFAfs UFO: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y8RfzkhqBLY

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jan 04, 2008 6:44 pm Yes, Gendo and Fuyutsuki were in fact inspired by that show. Sadamoto said so himself, and he said at least one particular shot in the opening was taken from it. EDIT: I don't mean their personalities were inspired by it, I mean their drawing designs were. Also, thanks for posting the actual theme here, as it's easy to highlight the similarities to NGE's opening, particularly the flashing words.

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Fri Jan 04, 2008 6:46 pm 70's GALORE! I think the similarity is too general to say that the Eva opening was inspired by this. Besides, Anno would have to be going for the most obscure homage ever if it‘s true.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jan 05, 2008 2:25 pm BUT SADAMOTO SAID...

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Jan 05, 2008 2:32 pm it's entirely possible, though IMO not probable and what Skullraper said

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 05, 2008 2:34 pm Younger Gendo is definitely modelled on Straker, so there may be something more to this.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jan 05, 2008 3:48 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: it's entirely possible, though IMO not probable

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Jan 05, 2008 4:10 pm SAUCE plz, NAveryW NOW

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jan 05, 2008 4:13 pm It's somewhere in the Onegeeks archives. I'm not going back in there again.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Jan 05, 2008 4:26 pm you should

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Jan 05, 2008 4:29 pm Someone should compile a list of obscure sci-fi shows/movies that Anno hasn't referenced. It would probably be shorter than the converse.
